title: Michael Phelps Makes Olympic History
story: Splash! Michael Phelps dove into the pool, chasing his eighth gold medal. On August 17, 2008, Phelps and his teammates Aaron Peirsol, Brendan Hansen, and Jason Lezak swam their hearts out in the 4 x 100 m medley relay at the Beijing Olympics. They not only won but also set a new world record! This victory gave Phelps his eighth gold medal of the Games, breaking Mark Spitz's 1972 record of seven.
activity_question: What record did Michael Phelps break in the 2008 Beijing Olympics?
activity_choices: Mark Spitz's 1972 record of seven gold medals
The world record for the 4 x 100 m medley relay
The record for most Olympic medals ever won
The record for fastest individual swim time
activity_answer: Mark Spitz's 1972 record of seven gold medals
follow_up_question: How many teammates helped Michael Phelps win the relay?

title: Cosmic Crash Caught on Camera
story: Boom! Two massive stars smashed together in space, and scientists caught it on camera. In 2017, a special observatory called LIGO detected ripples in space caused by this cosmic collision. It was the first time humans ever saw two neutron stars - super dense leftovers from exploded stars - crash into each other. This discovery on August 17 helped unlock secrets about how heavy elements like gold are made in the universe.

title: Tiny Moon Discovered Around Mars
story: Peering through a powerful telescope, astronomer Asaph Hall made an exciting discovery in 1877. He spotted a tiny moon orbiting Mars! This moon, later named Phobos, had been hiding from view until August 17. Hall's sharp eyes and patience paid off, revealing one of Mars's secrets. Phobos is so small that it would fit inside many Earth cities. This finding helped scientists learn more about our mysterious red neighbor in space.

bonus_fact: Phobos is shaped like a potato and is slowly getting closer to Mars over time.

title: Venera 7: First Spacecraft to Land on Venus
story: Blast off! On August 17, 1970, the Soviet Union launched Venera 7, a spacecraft destined for Venus. It survived the fiery descent and became the first human-made object to transmit from the surface of another planet.

title: Red Bull's Creator Takes Flight
story: A fizzy future was born in Thailand on August 17, 1923. Chaleo Yoovidhya entered the world, destined to give it wings. This enterprising boy from Phichit would grow up to co-create Red Bull, the world-famous energy drink. His invention would eventually buzz across the globe, energizing millions.
activity_question: What famous drink did Chaleo Yoovidhya help create?
follow_up_question: Why do you think energy drinks became so popular around the world?

title: The Birth of a Beloved Cartoonist
story: Pencils and paper came to life when Jean-Jacques Sempé was born in 1932. This French artist would grow up to create the charming world of Le Petit Nicolas, delighting readers with his whimsical cartoons. Sempé's journey began on August 17 in Pessac, France, where his talent for capturing life's funny moments was just waiting to be discovered.
activity_question: What famous cartoon series did Jean-Jacques Sempé create?
follow_up_question: Can you imagine what kind of funny scenes Sempé might have drawn from his childhood?

title: A Royal Mystery: The Prince in the Tower
story: A young prince vanished into history on August 17, 1473. Richard of Shrewsbury, Duke of York, was born in England. As the son of King Edward IV, he seemed destined for greatness. But Richard became one of the famous 'princes in the tower' who mysteriously disappeared. What happened to the royal boy? The answer remains a puzzle that still intrigues people today.
activity_question: Who was Richard of Shrewsbury's father?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the disappearance of the princes in the tower is still a mystery today?
